body:
## Why this matters

The media-service path is the right first workload lane, but **GPU transcoding and hardware passthrough** will distort the base scope if they stay implicit. This needs a separate child issue so the initial media lane can stay implementable while hardware-specific follow-up remains visible.

## Scope

Split the hardware-heavy media requirements into their own issue:

- define the GPU transcoding / passthrough requirements that are out of scope for the base media-service lane
- capture where those requirements touch the substrate epic versus the service-workload epic
- require explicit follow-up for any host, VM, or device-plugin assumptions instead of burying them inside the first media lane
- keep the issue tied to lab validation and evidence, not product marketing

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] GPU transcoding / passthrough requirements are split clearly from the base media-service lane
- [ ] The issue states the substrate assumptions that must be proven before hardware-accelerated media validation can exist
- [ ] The dependency between service and substrate work is explicit
- [ ] The issue is linked to #51, #54, and the media lane in #59

## Links

- Child of #51 and #54
- Depends on #54 and #59
- Derived from the hardware-transcoding risk surfaced while mining `projectbluefin/bluespeed#4` and `#1`
title: feat: split GPU transcoding and passthrough from the base media lane
